Restrict the trace to instructions whose PC is in

. This isolates a
single routine (e.g. a depacker loop) and, by excluding interrupt handlers,
yields a contiguous deterministic stream that lines up across emulators.

. Each hit includes the D7 alert code decoded with the

One-shot memory dump the first time the debugger activates: LEN bytes
from hex address ADDR are written to FILE, read through the CPU’s own
memory decode so chip-RAM mirrors resolve. Combined with AFTER, this
captures bitplane or sample data exactly as displayed at a moment in
time for offline analysis.
One-shot Copper-list disassembly the first time the debugger activates.

reads the live COP1LC; an explicit address disassembles from
there. COUNT defaults to 256 instructions (

Activity window in emulated seconds. Outside the window the debugger is
inert, which keeps traces focused and runs fast: combined with
determinism, you can binary-search a failure in time.

Beyond the debugger, many subsystems have start-up diagnostic switches.
They are read through

; grep its call sites for the
authoritative list. The most useful ones:
Per-colour-clock chip-bus owner map for a frame (
Blitter slot trace: one stderr line per blitter pipeline cycle (
BLTP frame vpos hpos TICK phase bus=0/1
), plus per-cck owner lines while a blit is in flight and
markers. Formatted for side-by-side diffing against a vAmiga build instrumented with the matching
CPU cycles spent per interrupt level
Copper list length (optionally at a given emulated time)
Every Copper MOVE’s landing colour clock (beam position, register, value), for cross-emulator write-landing comparison against vAmiga’s
CPU chip-bus access request/grant/end slots for fetch, chip/slow RAM, and custom space; optional
filters by CPU-visible addresses including custom registers such as
CPU custom-register reads’ granted chip-bus slot and returned value, plus the post-flush beam position; honors the same optional

Every CPU custom-register write’s granted chip-bus slot and effect beam position (register, value), the CPU-side companion of

: per-line bitplane capture state at DDF start, including DMACON, current and DDF-anchor BPLCON0, FMODE/DIW/DDF, effective fetch window, unit/period/quantum, words/row, modulos, and all BPLxPTs -- separates wrong-pointer from wrong-decode display bugs
: log beam-timed COLOR writes for selected beam lines, including source, framebuffer x, palette entry, LOCT, value, and BPLCON3; the setting is cached after first use

: sample DMA playfield HAM pixels on one beam line, including framebuffer/native x, selected bitplane index, active/fetched state, HAM hold colour before/after, output latch, plane count, fetched width, BPLCON1 delays, DIW/DDF, and display window; pairs with
and is cached after first use

: sample CPU/Copper BPLDAT replay pixels on one beam line, including source x/native bit, selected index, HAM seed/output state, output latch, BPLCON0/BPLCON1, and display window; cached after first use
: sample final framebuffer pixels after playfield, manual BPLDAT replay, sprites, and final blanking so post-decode overwrites can be isolated; cached after first use
: log every captured sprite DMA line (frame, channel, hstart, attach, FMODE width, data words) on one beam line or all of them; also logs SPRxPT writes and active stream retargets
: log manually replayed sprite intervals, sprite register writes with CPU/Copper source, BPLCON3/BPLCON4/FMODE/COLOR timing, sprite pointer alignment, and held wide-sprite words

: sample non-transparent sprite pixels on one beam line, including sprite or attached-pair index, palette entry, sprite RGB, final framebuffer RGB, playfield mask, priority/display gates, DIW, BPLCON2, BPLCON3, and BPLCON4; STEP defaults to 32 framebuffer pixels
, skip sprite rendering in full-frame output while leaving playfield/manual-BPL rendering active; useful for isolating sprite-owned pixels in screenshots
(emulated seconds): log the full blitter register set at every blit start (classic BLTSIZE and ECS BLTSIZH); pairs with

Path to a JSONL trace of blitter starts, forced finishes, DMACONR polls, and completion IRQ latches; start records include minterm/control registers, DMA/display context, FMODE, and all eight bitplane pointers
CPU empty-RAM execution and low-memory blitter write context
A2091 SCSI board register traffic (DMAC + WD33C93 accesses; the trace that brings up boot-ROM issues)
On every mouse-button press, log the raw host cursor position, the window’s scale factor and inner size, the texture supersample factor, the
result, and which region (status bar / display / none) the click resolved to; for diagnosing mouse capture on DPI scale changes or mixed-scale monitors
on every BLTSIZE write between START and END emulated seconds; output goes to
Timing-model knobs that pair well with the debugger:

-- override the modelled Paula INTREQ-to-
IPL-pin pipe (default 5 colour clocks;

also disables the 68000
boundary-sampling delay, delivering interrupts immediately).

-- bound
debugger and renderer diagnostics to an emulated-time window. Renderer
diagnostics parse these bounds once when their diagnostic option is first
used.

-- disable presentation recentring when debugging
display alignment.

-- save screenshots and frame dumps as the raw
716x570 woven framebuffer instead of the TV PNG aperture (692x540 for
standard PAL fields) or full-overscan presentation scale. Per-scanline
forensics (which exact framebuffer row carries an artifact) need the raw
field.

maps one host
row per woven scanline (a standard PAL screen becomes an exact 2x2 of
its bitmap in screenshots and the window).

-- CRT phosphor persistence for one run
(overrides

-- force the synchronous renderer instead
of the default render worker when bisecting presentation or capture
issues.

feature. Normal builds ignore them so release runs
stay hardware-derived and reproducible.
